working_on: Health-check audit at S-0184 (cadence trigger overdue by 2; ADR 0057 adversarial-stance audit; window S-0163 → S-0183)

outcome_summary: Cadence-fired adversarial-stance audit landed end-to-end with inline user adjudication. Two non-obvious findings: A — MemPalace retrieval broken in three reinforcing ways (HNSW UNKNOWN; upstream wing-filter throws Issue #1; BM25 fallback returns mining pollution; ADR 0057 element 4's prescribed pushback/lesson cluster reading is structurally un-runnable in current substrate); B — persistent-warn surface saturated, 7 categories at ≥10 persistence, alarm-too-noisy-to-alert pattern. Two user-pre-named adjudications: Finding-MEMPALACE → option (c) settle Phase-6 recall-substrate decision NOW → Issue #131; Finding-ENGINE-LOG → option (d) cut 0.1.0 + structural split + duplication audit → Issue #132. Finding B → option B1 separate annotated-baselines bucket → Issue #133. Inline cleanup: STATE.md prune of accreted verification-prose rows S-0156/0157/0160/0163 (198 → 167 lines, back under 180 threshold). All five S-0162 findings closed at S-0163 — pipeline worked end-to-end. Cadence stays at 20. mempalace_unavailable_acknowledged: boot mempalace_search ran via CLI (mempalace search, venv binary) for the freshness probe rather than via mcp__mempalace__mempalace_search, so post-mempalace-tool-use hook did not log search/diary_read activity — same plan-mode-then-claim surface effect as S-0160/S-0162. Diary write + decision drawer landed via MCP and are tracked. validate.py + classifier interaction caused two pre-commit hangs that user resolved by killing zombie processes (PIDs 98390/98393, 1h50m old, from a prior session attempting to commit migration 0067 already landed in S-0183) + authorizing production DB reads inline.
next_session_handle: Three audit-spawned session-sized work items + PDG papers Session α (carried from S-0171 / HANDOFF.md). User adjudication picks order. Issue #131 (Phase-6 recall-substrate decision ADR) ideally lands BEFORE Session α since Session α depends on decision-drawer recall and the audit confirmed retrieval is broken. Issue #132 (ENGINE_LOG cut + split + duplication audit) and Issue #133 (persistent-warn annotated-baselines bucket) are independent, can interleave. PDG papers Session α — cross-reference audit against 89 ADRs, output engine/build_readiness/pdg_papers_extraction/adr_cross_reference_map.md.
mempalace_unavailable_acknowledged: boot mempalace_search ran via venv CLI binary (mempalace search) for the audit's freshness probe per ADR 0057 element 3, not via mcp__mempalace__mempalace_search — the post-mempalace-tool-use hook only logs MCP calls, so the CLI invocation is invisible to the activity tracker. Same plan-mode-then-claim surface effect as S-0160/S-0162; the search itself happened and produced the audit's gold-signal Finding A.
